title: Faroe Islands 🇫🇴 - by drone [4K]
creator: Drone Snap
notes: DRAFT CANDIDATE -- produced in a cloud (Cowork) session; not yet cross-checked against the live exemplars directory for duplicates at the time of drafting (a freshness check is performed immediately before commit). Third Faroe Islands candidate added this session, deliberately from a different creator/format than the two already-committed Faroe records (ecksplorer-faroe-mulafossur-waterfall.json, a single-landmark cinematic piece from a consumer-drone hobbyist, and scenicrelaxation-faroe-islands-fpv.json, a whole-archipelago FPV relaxation film) -- this one is a general whole-archipelago 4K piece from an explicitly team-run, multi-contributor drone channel, giving this location a third distinct production-model in the pack. Verified this session via WebFetch of the YouTube oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cD9HEBz_QPs&format=json), which returned title='Faroe Islands 🇫🇴 - by drone [4K]', author_name='Drone Snap', author_url='https://www.youtube.com/@DroneSnap', provider_name='YouTube' -- confirms a real, currently-public video with this exact title/creator. The watch-page fetch (for CC-license/upload-date/duration/description) returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) this session, consistent with every other candidate processed this session; not retried further or worked around, so `license_category` is the conservative all-rights-reserved default on absence of a positive CC finding -- re-check when reachable. A WebFetch of the channel page this session (https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCbDH6Ga-wuaam2IHqAxm3hg) returned real, specific, above-average corroboration for this batch: the channel is explicitly described as a four-person team (named as Sascha, Carrie, Frida, and Henry) that releases new drone videos weekly and runs a viewer-submission program for community-shot drone footage -- this is direct evidence (from the channel's own description, not a third-party inference) of a genuine, active, multi-person dedicated drone-content operation rather than a single hobbyist upload, though no numeric subscriber count was returned. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: no contest/broadcaster/festival attribution found. `scores` are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated`, calibrated at essentially the same tier as scenicrelaxation-faroe-islands-fpv.json (0.74/0.75/0.78, composite 0.7567) given a comparable profile of 'real, active, moderately-well-evidenced dedicated small operation, no confirmed numeric reach' -- the composite happens to round to the identical 0.7567 figure by coincidence of the underlying per-signal estimates, not by copying. `composite_score` is NOT a call to the repo's real `compute_composite_score`/`default_weights()` functions (no shell access to the user's local Python environment this session); it was computed via an equivalent equal-weighted-mean-of-present-signals formula run in this session's own Python: (0.75+0.75+0.77)/3 = 0.7566666666666667, rounded to 0.7567. Re-run through the real repo function before treating this figure as certified. `composition` stays null, matching Milestone 1 convention.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: Not independently reviewed via playback this session. The terse title ('by drone [4K]') gives less pacing signal than other records' titles in this pack; 'long-form-cinematic' is a default inference from the standard (non-/shorts/) watch URL rather than any observed cut/shot-length pattern -- this record's pacing_notes should be treated as the weakest-evidenced field in this batch and corrected first if a live-playback review becomes possible.
